components.name: Frontend UI
components.description: index.html + main.js + styles.css — vanilla JS, no framework, no bundler

components.name: Settings
components.description: settings.html (Tauri: separate WebviewWindow) or in-app modal (Web/Mobile). Syncs via localStorage + storage event.

components.name: Tauri Backend
components.description: src-tauri/lib.rs — Rust process: global PTT shortcut, presence_fetch proxy, deep-link handler, app menu

components.name: PeerJS Broker
components.description: Cloud signaling server — brokers WebRTC connections. Room code = host's PeerJS peer ID.

components.name: WebRTC (P2P)
components.description: DataConnection (signaling star: host↔peer) + MediaConnection (audio full mesh: every peer↔peer, Opus 16kHz mono)

components.name: Voxal Presence API
components.description: Supabase Edge Function — manages orgs, channels, sessions, ICE server config. Base URL from localStorage['service-url'].

components.name: Voxal Connect
components.description: voxal.app — OAuth-style auth frontend. Redirects back via voxal://auth?token=… or postMessage.

components.name: TURN / ICE Servers
components.description: Embed-config / org / metered.ca relay servers, falling back to public STUN + a best-effort free TURN relay. Credentials fetched at room creation/join.

components.name: localStorage
components.description: Persistent state: pseudo, PTT shortcut, presence token, org ID, TURN config, theme, metered status

components.name: iOS Native (Capacitor)
components.description: PTTPlugin.swift — PushToTalk framework, Dynamic Island, AVAudioSession, SceneDelegate for deep links

components.name: Android Native (Capacitor)
components.description: AudioForegroundPlugin.java — foreground service for audio focus, notification channel

components.name: iframe Parent
components.description: Optional embedding host page — controls Voxal via postMessage bridge (join/create/leave commands, state events)

flows.name: Create Room
flows.description: Host creates a new room and waits for peers to join

steps.label: getMicStream()
steps.detail: Requests microphone access (Opus, 16kHz mono, echo cancellation). Uses webkitGetUserMedia fallback.

steps.label: fetchIceServers()
steps.detail: Tries: 0) embed postMessage 'config' ICE servers, 1) org ICE servers from Presence API, 2) local metered.ca credentials, 3) public STUN + free TURN relay fallback (localStorage['turn-fallback'] override)

steps.label: new Peer({ config: { iceServers } })
steps.detail: Connects to PeerJS broker with ICE config. Receives a random peer ID which becomes the room code.

steps.label: peer.on('open', id)
steps.detail: PeerJS assigns a unique ID. This ID IS the room code. Sets isHost=true, roomCode=id, inRoom=true.

steps.label: localStorage.setItem('active-room-code', id)
steps.detail: Persists room code for potential rejoin after crash/refresh.

steps.label: showScreen('room')
steps.detail: Switches UI to room view. Starts heartbeat broadcast, stats polling, keep-alive audio.

steps.label: nativePTTJoin(roomCode)
steps.detail: Joins the PushToTalk channel on iOS — enables Dynamic Island PTT button.

steps.label: requestAudioFocus()
steps.detail: Starts foreground service to keep audio focus while in room.

flows.name: Join Room
flows.description: Peer joins an existing room by entering the room code (host's peer ID)

steps.label: getMicStream()
steps.detail: Acquires mic if not already held.

steps.label: fetchIceServers()
steps.detail: Fetches TURN credentials (org → metered → STUN fallback).

steps.label: new Peer({ config: { iceServers } })
steps.detail: Registers with PeerJS broker. Gets own random peer ID.

steps.label: peer.connect(hostId, { reliable: true })
steps.detail: Opens a DataConnection to the host (room code = host's peer ID).

steps.label: hostData.on('open')
steps.detail: DataConnection established with host.

steps.label: hostData.send({ type: 'hello', pseudo })
steps.detail: Joiner sends its display name to the host.

steps.label: msg.type === 'peer-list'
steps.detail: Host replies with full peer roster: [{id, pseudo}], hostId, deputyId, successorIds. This confirms join success.

steps.label: finishJoin(hostId, hostData)
steps.detail: Sets roomCode, inRoom=true, starts heartbeat monitor, shows room screen.

steps.label: peer.call(peerId, stream) — for each peer
steps.detail: Opens MediaConnection (audio) to every peer in the room. Full mesh topology.

flows.name: Push-to-Talk (Desktop)
flows.description: User presses the global PTT shortcut on macOS/Windows/Linux

steps.label: Global shortcut detected (default: Shift+Space)
steps.detail: tauri-plugin-global-shortcut captures system-wide key press, even when app is not focused.

steps.label: app.emit('ptt-press')
steps.detail: Rust backend emits ptt-press event to the WebView via Tauri IPC.

steps.label: setTalking(true)
steps.detail: Enables audioTrack, plays blip sound, updates UI (#ptt-btn.active, status text).

steps.label: broadcastTalkingState(true)
steps.detail: Sends { type: 'talking', peerId, active: true } over DataConnection. Host relays to all peers.

steps.label: Audio flows via MediaConnection
steps.detail: Opus-encoded audio is transmitted P2P to all connected peers via the full mesh.

steps.label: app.emit('ptt-release')
steps.detail: Key released → ptt-release event → setTalking(false) → audioTrack.enabled = false.

flows.name: Push-to-Talk (iOS)
flows.description: User presses the Dynamic Island PTT button on iOS

steps.label: Dynamic Island PTT button pressed
steps.detail: iOS PushToTalk framework detects button press on Dynamic Island / Lock Screen.

steps.label: didBeginTransmittingFrom callback
steps.detail: PTChannelManager delegate fires. Must resume AudioContext before setting gain.

steps.label: evaluateJavaScript('audioCtx.resume()…gainNode.gain.value=1')
steps.detail: Swift evaluates JS in WebView to unmute mic via GainNode (not audioTrack.enabled — Swift controls gain directly).

steps.label: Audio flows through GainNode → MediaConnection
steps.detail: Mic audio, routed through Web Audio API GainNode, is transmitted to peers.

steps.label: didEndTransmittingFrom → gainNode.gain.value=0
steps.detail: Button released → Swift mutes the GainNode. JS engine may be suspended in background — gain control is synchronous.

flows.name: Host Migration
flows.description: When the host disconnects, peers elect a successor and reconnect signaling

steps.label: Host heartbeat timeout / DataConnection close
steps.detail: Non-host peer detects host death via missing heartbeat (bidirectional) or DataConnection 'close' event.

steps.label: initiateHostMigration(oldHostId)
steps.detail: Sets roomState='migrating'. Cleans up old host's data+media connections. Idempotent via state machine.

steps.label: proceedWithHostElection()
steps.detail: Uses authoritativeSuccessorIds from last host peer-list/heartbeat. Skips _migrationExcluded candidates.

steps.label: Elected peer: becomeHost()
steps.detail: Sets isHost=true, roomCode=self, starts host heartbeat. Preserves knownPeerIds during settle window (8s).

steps.label: Other peers: connectToNewHost(newHostId)
steps.detail: Opens new DataConnection to elected host. Retries up to 3 times before re-electing.

steps.label: New host sends peer-list
steps.detail: Migration succeeds when the new host's authoritative peer-list arrives. Audio mesh is NOT touched — survives handoff.

steps.label: broadcastHostPeerLists() after settle
steps.detail: After 8s settle timeout, new host prunes peers that never reconnected and broadcasts clean roster.

flows.name: Connect Voxal Account
flows.description: OAuth-style authentication flow to link a Voxal account for presence/channels

steps.label: connectWithVoxalAccount()
steps.detail: Generates random state parameter, stores in sessionStorage for CSRF protection.

steps.label: Open voxal.app/connect?state=…
steps.detail: Tauri: shell.open() in system browser. Capacitor: window.open(_system). Web: popup window.

steps.label: User authenticates
steps.detail: User logs in or creates account on the Voxal Connect web app.

steps.label: Redirect: voxal://auth?token=…&state=…
steps.detail: Desktop: deep-link caught by Tauri plugin. iOS: appUrlOpen event. Web: postMessage to opener window.

steps.label: handleDeepLink(url)
steps.detail: Validates state parameter. Stores token in localStorage. Triggers org selection.

steps.label: localStorage.setItem('presence-api-token', token)
steps.detail: Persists auth token for future API calls.

steps.label: selectOrgAndStartPolling()
steps.detail: Fetches user's orgs, selects first/saved org, starts channel polling.

flows.name: Join Presence Channel
flows.description: User selects a channel from their org's channel list

steps.label: GET /session?org_id=…
steps.detail: Polls for channel list + connected peers. Response includes channel names and peer_ids of connected users.

steps.label: Channel data
steps.detail: Returns array of { channel: {name}, connected: [{peer_id, pseudo}] }.

steps.label: renderPresenceChannels()
steps.detail: Renders channel buttons in the home screen presence panel.

steps.label: User clicks a channel
steps.detail: joinChannel(item) — if no peers connected, createRoom(). If peers exist, joinRoom(firstPeerId).

steps.label: POST /session (channelName, peerId)
steps.detail: Registers this peer in the presence system so others can see them in the channel.

steps.label: createRoom() or joinRoom(hostId)
steps.detail: Proceeds with standard room creation or joining flow.

flows.name: Share / Invite to Room
flows.description: Host shares the room code or deep link so others can join

steps.label: Copy room code button
steps.detail: Copies the room code (host's peer ID) to clipboard. Uses fallbackCopy() on iOS WebView.

steps.label: Share button → navigator.share()
steps.detail: Uses Web Share API with voxal://join?room=<code> in the text field (not url field — iOS rejects non-http schemes).

steps.label: Recipient opens deep link
steps.detail: voxal://join?room=<peerId> — handled by handleDeepLink() which calls joinRoom(roomId).

steps.label: publishRoom() — if presence configured
steps.detail: Optionally publishes room to the anonymous-rooms API so it appears in channel listings.

flows.name: Open Settings
flows.description: User opens preferences to configure PTT shortcut, TURN, theme, etc.

steps.label: openSettings()
steps.detail: Platform detection determines settings UI mode.

steps.label: Tauri: new WebviewWindow('preferences', { url: 'settings.html' })
steps.detail: Opens a separate native window loading settings.html. Shares localStorage with main window.

steps.label: Web/Mobile: show #modal-settings
steps.detail: Reveals the in-app settings modal overlay. Populates fields from localStorage.

steps.label: User changes a setting
steps.detail: settings.html writes to localStorage (pseudo, theme, TURN config, etc.).

steps.label: window.addEventListener('storage', …)
steps.detail: Main window receives storage event when settings.html modifies localStorage. Applies changes live.

steps.label: invoke('update_ptt_shortcut', { shortcut })
steps.detail: Tauri desktop only: re-registers the global keyboard shortcut via Rust IPC command.

flows.name: ICE / TURN Setup
flows.description: Layered fallback for fetching relay server credentials (embed config → org → metered.ca → STUN + free relay)

steps.label: 1. GET /org/{orgId}/ice-servers
steps.detail: If presenceConfigured(), tries org-managed ICE servers first. Header: x-api-token.

steps.label: { ice_servers: [...] } or empty
steps.detail: Returns TURN/STUN server array if configured for the org. Falls through if empty/error.

steps.label: 2. GET metered.ca credentials
steps.detail: If local metered-app-name + metered-api-key are in localStorage, fetches from metered.ca API.

steps.label: TURN server credentials
steps.detail: Returns array of ICE server objects with URLs and credentials.

steps.label: 3. STUN + free TURN relay fallback
steps.detail: If no org/metered TURN, uses Google STUN plus a best-effort free public TURN relay (overridable via localStorage['turn-fallback'] / Settings → Advanced → Fallback relay). STUN alone fails behind symmetric NAT / firewalls; the relay (TCP/443 + TLS) traverses them.

steps.label: Cache TURN status
steps.detail: Stores metered-status ('ok'/'error'), metered-count, and metered-servers in localStorage for badge display.

flows.name: Leave Room
flows.description: User leaves the current room — cleans up all connections and state

steps.label: unpublishRoom() + deleteSession()
steps.detail: Removes room from anonymous-rooms API and deletes presence session if channel was active.

steps.label: leaveRoom()
steps.detail: Sets inRoom=false, stops heartbeats, stats polling, keep-alive. Clears knownPeerIds.

steps.label: Close all DataConnections + MediaConnections
steps.detail: Iterates connections map, closes each peer's data and media links.

steps.label: peer.destroy()
steps.detail: Disconnects from PeerJS broker, releases the peer ID.

steps.label: Stop mic stream
steps.detail: stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop()). Releases microphone hardware.

steps.label: nativePTTLeave()
steps.detail: Leaves the PushToTalk channel — removes Dynamic Island button.

steps.label: releaseAudioFocus()
steps.detail: Stops the foreground service and releases audio focus.

steps.label: localStorage.removeItem('active-room-code')
steps.detail: Clears the persisted room code. Prevents stale rejoin attempts.

steps.label: showScreen('home')
steps.detail: Returns to the home screen.

flows.name: iframe Embed Control
flows.description: Parent page controls an embedded Voxal instance via postMessage

steps.label: postMessage({ type: 'join', roomCode })
steps.detail: Parent page sends join command. Also supports 'create' and 'leave' types.

steps.label: joinRoom(roomCode) or createRoom()
steps.detail: Voxal iframe processes the command and joins/creates the room.

steps.label: iframeEmit({ source: 'voxal', type: 'joined', roomCode, peerId })
steps.detail: Voxal notifies parent of state changes: joined, left, talking, peers, host-changed.

flows.name: Deep Link Join
flows.description: User clicks a voxal://join?room=… link to join a room directly

steps.label: deep-link://new-url event
steps.detail: Tauri deep-link plugin catches voxal:// URL on macOS. Emits event to WebView.

steps.label: appUrlOpen event
steps.detail: iOS SceneDelegate routes voxal:// URL to Capacitor which fires appUrlOpen in JS.

steps.label: handleDeepLink('voxal://join?room=…')
steps.detail: Parses URL. If hostname='join', extracts room code. Leaves current room if needed.

steps.label: joinRoom(roomId)
steps.detail: Standard join flow — getMicStream → PeerJS → connectToHost → finishJoin.

flows.name: Change PTT Shortcut
flows.description: User customizes the push-to-talk keyboard shortcut (Tauri desktop only)

steps.label: User records new shortcut
steps.detail: Settings UI captures keydown event and formats it as a shortcut string (e.g. 'Ctrl+M').

steps.label: localStorage.setItem('ptt-shortcut', newShortcut)
steps.detail: Persists the new shortcut string.

steps.label: invoke('update_ptt_shortcut', { shortcut })
steps.detail: Tauri IPC call to Rust backend.

steps.label: Unregister old → register new global shortcut
steps.detail: Rust unregisters the previous shortcut and registers the new one system-wide. Returns Result<(), String>.

flows.name: CORS Proxy (Tauri)
flows.description: Tauri desktop proxies HTTP requests through Rust to bypass WebView CORS restrictions

steps.label: tauriFetch(url, options)
steps.detail: JS wrapper that detects Tauri and routes through IPC instead of browser fetch.

steps.label: invoke('presence_fetch', { url, method, token, body })
steps.detail: Sends HTTP request details to Rust via Tauri IPC.

steps.label: reqwest::Client → GET/POST/DELETE
steps.detail: Rust makes the actual HTTP request using reqwest. Adds x-api-token and x-room-secret headers.

steps.label: JSON response
steps.detail: API response flows back through Rust.

steps.label: serde_json::Value → JS
steps.detail: Rust parses response as JSON and returns it to the WebView via IPC.
